text: The robotic instruments, which mimic the bending of fingers and hands, are used to disconnect the blood supply from the portion of the stomach to be removed. A vessel sealer is employed to simultaneously seal and cut the blood vessels, ensuring precise operation without bleeding.

The surgeon manipulates the upper portion of the stomach, releasing it from surrounding tissues near the diaphragm and spleen. The reddish spleen is visible to the right, while the diaphragm is positioned behind the stomach. The goal is to allow the esophagus to descend through the diaphragm to connect with the stomach.

The surgeon completes the dissection along the greater curvature of the stomach using a robotic instrument, which bends to facilitate precise manipulation. The dissection is performed with minimal bleeding. The surgeon inspects behind the freed greater curvature to confirm the absence of adhesions or connections to the underlying pancreas.

The surgeon places a tube into the stomach to size the sleeve, ensuring proper positioning. The da Vinci robot's smart stapler is then introduced to measure the tissue thickness and verify the appropriate staple length for the transection.

The surgeon uses a robotic stapler to conform to the patient's anatomy, snugly positioning it against the tube to ensure precise removal of the majority of the stomach. The sleeve is visible on the left side, while the portion of the stomach to be removed is on the right side.

The surgeon uses the robotic stapler to perform the final firing, completing the sleeve gastrectomy by removing the remaining portion of the stomach. The technology provides a clear view of the remaining stomach tissue before the stapler is fired.

The surgeon sets aside the removed portion of the stomach and prepares for a leak test. The staple line is irrigated, and air is blown under pressure to check for leaks, with no bubbling or leaking observed.
The surgeon removes the extra fluid from the surgical field, extracts the tube from the stomach, and places the resected portion of the stomach into a bag for removal through the abdominal wall.
